title: Validate Fresh Install (Dry Run)
summary: Validation revealed critical blocker: extensive personal identifiers still present in codebase. System not ready for public release.

hardcoded_paths.details: Hardcoded '/home/workspace' paths only found in acceptable defaults in meeting_config.py and pulse_common.py (both use environment variables with fallback)

personal_identifiers.details: Found 100+ instances of personal data across N5 codebase including:
findings: Google Drive folder IDs: Multiple hardcoded folder IDs
Persona UUIDs: Multiple hardcoded persona identifiers
Test data with real names and emails
personal_identifiers.impact: Codebase contains extensive personal/business data that must be sanitized before public release

affected_components: Test files with real email addresses and names
Meeting examples with real participant names
CRM scripts with personal email addresses
required_actions: Systematic sanitization of all personal identifiers
Replace real emails with example.com addresses in tests
Replace real names with placeholder names
Review and sanitize all example data

critical_blockers: Personal data must be sanitized before public release
Environment variables needed for service IDs
recommendations: Run comprehensive personal data cleanup pass
Create sanitization script to automate cleanup
Add validation checks to prevent personal data commits
Review all example/test data for anonymization
notes: While core functionality imports correctly and hardcoded paths are properly handled, the extensive personal data contamination is a critical blocker for public release. This requires systematic sanitization before the codebase can be safely made public.
